name: ExtensionMeasure_Group_Stratifier_GroupDefinition
title: R5: A group resource that defines this population (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Measure.group.stratifier.groupDefinition` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Measure.group.stratifier.groupDefinition` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Measure.group.stratifier.groupDefinition` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Measure.group.stratifier.groupDefinition` has a context of Measure.group.stratifier based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.group.stratifier.groupDefinition` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: A group resource that defines this population (new)
element.comment:
Element `Measure.group.stratifier.groupDefinition` has a context of Measure.group.stratifier based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.group.stratifier.groupDefinition` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Note that this approach can only be used for measures where the basis is of the same type as the populations (e.g. a Patient-based measure, rather than an Encounter-based measure). In addition, the referenced Group SHALL be a definitional (as opposed to an actual) group.

element.short: A group resource that defines this population
element.definition: A Group resource that defines this population as a set of characteristics.
element.comment: Note that this approach can only be used for measures where the basis is of the same type as the populations (e.g. a Patient-based measure, rather than an Encounter-based measure). In addition, the referenced Group SHALL be a definitional (as opposed to an actual) group.
